sizing_rule.note: Size positions using 1.25R stop loss width. Rule emphasizes that traders must tolerate 10-15 trades minimum to achieve profitability given the stop loss width chosen. Tighter stops reduce win rate; wider stops preserve winning trades but increase loss magnitude. Position size should be calibrated so that the 1.25R stop loss aligns with minimum 40% win rate over the trade cycle.

source_rule_text: A trader should balance stop loss tightness against system reliability, recognizing that tighter stops reduce win rate and require more trades to achieve profitability, while wider stops preserve capital on winning trades but increase single-trade loss magnitude.
